restaurants.name: Bebek Bengil
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf

restaurants.name: Bebek Tepi Sawah
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali - All Inclusive Guide - Prashita.pdf

restaurants.name: Green Kubu Cafe
restaurants.ambience: Panoramic views over lush paddy fields
highlights: Panoramic views over lush paddy fields
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali - All Inclusive Guide - Prashita.pdf

restaurants.name: Jukung Grill at Bali Masari
restaurants.ambience: Ideal for a slow, riverside evening meal
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali - All Inclusive Guide - Prashita.pdf

restaurants.name: Jendela Bali – The Panoramic Resto
restaurants.ambience: Elevated views and breezy outdoor deck within GWK park
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali - All Inclusive Guide - Prashita.pdf

restaurants.name: Kampung Bali Restaurant
restaurants.ambience: Rustic ambiance and traditional Balinese buffet setup
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali - All Inclusive Guide - Prashita.pdf

restaurants.name: Samabe Cave Dining
restaurants.ambience: Exclusive candlelit dinner in a seaside cave (pre-booking required)

attractions.name: Twin Lake Viewpoint
attractions.description: Dramatic viewpoint overlooking Lake Buyan and Lake Tamblingan — two crater lakes separated by a lush green ridge
attractions.entry_fee: Usually free or ₹50-₹100 for certain photo spots
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf

attractions.name: Ulun Danu Beratan Temple
attractions.description: The temple appears to float on the calm waters of Lake Beratan with mountains rising behind it
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf

attractions.name: Lempuyang Temple (Gateway to Heaven)
attractions.description: One of Bali's oldest and most sacred temples, sits high in the eastern mountains with Mount Agung framed between temple's split gates
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf

attractions.name: Bali Safari & Marine Park
attractions.description: Get close to exotic animals in a lush safari setting
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf
Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Priyankee and Varun.pdf

attractions.name: Tanah Lot Temple
attractions.description: One of Bali's most photographed sea temples perched dramatically on a rock formation, especially magical at sunset
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Desmond.pdf
Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Priyankee and Varun.pdf

attractions.name: Uluwatu Temple
attractions.description: Sacred temple built on a dramatic cliffside 70 meters above the sea, offers panoramic ocean views and forested paths
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Desmond.pdf
Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Priyankee and Varun.pdf

attractions.name: Kecak Fire Dance
attractions.description: Hypnotic performance featuring chorus of chanting men, fire effects, and the legendary tale of Ramayana set against sunset backdrop
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Desmond.pdf
Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Priyankee and Varun.pdf

attractions.name: Ayung River
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali - All Inclusive Guide - Prashita.pdf

attractions.name: Outback Adventure
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali - All Inclusive Guide - Prashita.pdf

attractions.name: Aloha Bali Swing
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali - All Inclusive Guide - Prashita.pdf

attractions.name: Garuda Wisnu Kencana (GWK) Cultural Park
attractions.description: Marvel at the 121-meter statue of Lord Vishnu riding Garuda, explore art exhibits, open-air plazas, and Balinese sculptures
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali - All Inclusive Guide - Prashita.pdf

attractions.name: Bali Swing
attractions.description: Iconic jungle swings that send you soaring above deep green valleys

attractions.name: Tegalalang Rice Terraces
attractions.description: The Tegalalang valley unfolds in sweeping curves of layered green — a masterpiece of traditional Subak irrigation

attractions.name: Kintamani Viewpoint (Mount Batur & Lake Batur)
attractions.description: Dramatic volcano–lake panorama with Mount Batur rising sharply from the valley

attractions.name: Tegenungan Waterfall
attractions.description: Tegenungan cascades beautifully into a rocky pool surrounded by dense foliage

attractions.name: Tukad Cepung Waterfall
attractions.description: Hidden waterfall inside a cave with dramatic light beams streaming through cliff opening

local_dishes.name: Nasi Ayam
local_dishes.description: Chicken rice comprised of shredded chicken, satay, boiled egg, roasted peanuts, french bean salad, sambal, and a bowl of steamed rice
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf
Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Priyankee and Varun.pdf

local_dishes.name: Ayam Betutu
local_dishes.description: Balinese spiced chicken - A bowl of steamed rice accompanied by chunks of chicken bathed in chilli sambal with the satay, salad and peanuts
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf
Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Priyankee and Varun.pdf

local_dishes.name: Lawar
local_dishes.description: Primarily made from coconut gratings, to which minced jackfruit, bits of chicken skin, chilies, and coconut milk are added
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf
Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Priyankee and Varun.pdf

local_dishes.name: Martabak
local_dishes.description: Popular Indonesian street-style pancake in savory chicken version or sweet chocolate & nut version
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf
Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Priyankee and Varun.pdf

local_dishes.name: Acai Bowls
local_dishes.description: Fresh customizable Acai Bowls and Smoothies are becoming popular
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf

local_dishes.name: Klepon
local_dishes.description: Indonesia Sweet Rice Cakes - Made from boiled rice cakes filled with palm sugar and covered in coconut gratings
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf

local_dishes.name: Tipat Cantok
local_dishes.description: Balinese Rice Cake salad - made from peanut sauce, rice cakes steamed in palm leaves, cabbage and baby green sprouts, mixed thoroughly and drizzled with sweet soy sauce
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf

local_dishes.name: Nasi Goreng
local_dishes.description: Various spices are mixed to form a thick paste, added to boiled rice and cooked on a slow flame. Loads of vegetables are added along with chicken or other meat
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf
Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Priyankee and Varun.pdf

local_dishes.name: Sambal Matah
local_dishes.description: Balinese Raw Sambal - prepared using finely chopped shallots, lemongrass, spices, garlic, and herbs, fried in hot oil with a sprinkle of shrimp paste
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf
Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Priyankee and Varun.pdf

local_dishes.name: Bubur Sumsum
local_dishes.description: Indonesian Rice Pudding - usually syrupy and served hot, garnished with coconut cream and sliced bananas
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf

local_dishes.name: Bebek Betutu
local_dishes.description: Balinese Roast Duck - prepared using a whole duck or chicken stuffed with vegetables, spices and herbs. Takes about 8-12 hours to prepare
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf
Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Priyankee and Varun.pdf

local_dishes.name: Jukut Ares
local_dishes.description: Balinese Traditional Soup - Salt is added to a finely chopped banana stalk, and the mixture is pressed to remove moisture. Turmeric and bay leaves, among other spices, are added along with chunks of chicken
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf

local_dishes.name: Pisang Goreng
local_dishes.description: Indonesian fried banana - can be paired with ice cream, syrup, chocolate, whipped cream, or eaten as it is
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf

local_dishes.name: Gado Gado
local_dishes.description: Indonesian salad - made of potatoes, beans, capsicum and tomatoes, with a peanut sauce dressing
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf

local_dishes.name: Fish Head Soup
local_dishes.description: Considered a delicacy, the fish head soup cooks the whole fish which are boiled in a broth to which various spices are added
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf
Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Priyankee and Varun.pdf

local_dishes.name: Serombotan
local_dishes.description: Mixture of various items starting with bean sprouts, spinach, green banana slices, soybeans, and peanuts, topped with coconut and peanut sauce
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf

local_dishes.name: Kopi Tubruk
local_dishes.description: Balinese Coffee
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf

local_dishes.name: Rujak
local_dishes.description: Indonesian Fruit Salad - Green mango, papaya, and yam beans are diced and mixed with shrimp paste, drizzled with chili sauce and dressed with palm sugar
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf

local_dishes.name: Es Cendol
local_dishes.description: Made from rice flour jelly, coconut milk, and palm sugar droplets. Occasionally, ice or crushed ice cubes may be added to cool down the drink
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf

local_dishes.name: Ikan Bakar
local_dishes.description: Grilled Fish
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf
Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Priyankee and Varun.pdf

local_dishes.name: Tum Ayan
local_dishes.description: Minced chicken mixed with flavorful spices and coconut milk, wrapped in banana leaves, and steamed or grilled. Contains minced chicken, shallots, garlic, coconut milk, spice paste, chili, and peppercorn
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf
Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Priyankee and Varun.pdf

local_dishes.name: Gado-Gado
local_dishes.description: Hearty Indonesian salad of steamed vegetables, tofu, tempeh, and boiled eggs topped with creamy peanut sauce
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Priyankee and Varun.pdf

local_dishes.name: Nasi Campur (Vegetarian Version)
local_dishes.description: A perfect way to taste multiple Balinese flavors in one plate

local_dishes.name: Mie Goreng (Vegetarian)
local_dishes.description: Classic Indonesian fried noodles prepared with vegetables, tofu/tempeh, and sweet–savory sauces

local_dishes.name: Nasi Goreng (Vegetarian)
local_dishes.description: Fried rice with veggies, egg (optional), tempeh, and sambal. Often served with crackers and pickles on the side

local_dishes.name: Sate Lilit Vegetarian
local_dishes.description: A plant-based version of Bali's satay using tempeh, mushrooms, tofu, or soy-based mix wrapped around lemongrass sticks and grilled

local_dishes.name: Tahu & Tempeh Goreng
local_dishes.description: Crispy fried tofu and tempeh served with chili dip. Simple but very satisfying

local_dishes.name: Sayur Lodeh
local_dishes.description: Traditional Indonesian coconut curry with vegetables like eggplant, beans, carrots, and tofu. Creamy, mild, and delicious
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Priyankee and Varun.pdf

local_dishes.name: Urab (Vegetable Salad)
local_dishes.description: A fresh mix of steamed vegetables with spiced grated coconut. A must-try Balinese side dish

local_dishes.name: Sweet Potato Fries / Purple Yam Snacks
local_dishes.description: Bali uses a lot of purple yam — often turned into fries, chips, or steamed snacks

local_dishes.name: Vegetarian Laksa (Indonesian Style)
local_dishes.description: Rich, aromatic coconut broth with noodles, tofu, and vegetables. Not Balinese originally, but very popular across Bali

local_dishes.name: Pisang Goreng (Banana fritters)
local_dishes.description: Banana fritters

local_dishes.name: Bubur Injin (Black rice pudding with coconut milk)
local_dishes.description: Black rice pudding with coconut milk

local_dishes.name: Nasi Campur
local_dishes.description: Traditional Indonesian mixed rice platter served with small portions of vegetables, sambal, tofu/tempeh, and choice of chicken

local_dishes.name: Chicken Satay (Sate Ayam)
local_dishes.description: Grilled skewers of marinated chicken served with rich peanut sauce and rice cakes

local_dishes.name: Lamb Rendang
local_dishes.description: Slow-cooked lamb simmered in coconut milk and aromatic spices until tender and deeply flavorful

local_dishes.name: Mie Goreng
local_dishes.description: Indonesia's famous stir-fried noodles with vegetables, egg, and choice of chicken
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Priyankee and Varun.pdf

local_dishes.name: Tempeh & Tofu Dishes
local_dishes.description: Plant-based dishes using locally made tempeh and tofu, often stir-fried, grilled, or served in mild curries

local_dishes.name: Sate Lilit
local_dishes.description: Traditional Balinese satay

local_dishes.name: Cap Cay
local_dishes.description: A Chinese-Indonesian stir-fry dish with a medley of vegetables like cabbage, carrots, baby corn, mushrooms, and more. It's often vegetarian by default
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Priyankee and Varun.pdf

local_dishes.name: Tahu Berontak
local_dishes.description: A popular and addictive snack of deep-fried tofu stuffed with vegetables like cabbage, carrots, and bean sprouts
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Priyankee and Varun.pdf

local_dishes.name: Dadar Gulung
local_dishes.description: Traditional green pancakes infused with pandan extract, filled with sweet, shredded coconut and palm sugar
source_files: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Priyankee and Varun.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Beware of overcharging taxis—use Grab/Gojek where allowed
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Keep valuables close; use anti-theft bags in crowded areas
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Avoid isolated beaches after dark
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Always remove shoes when entering temples and homes
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Dress modestly in religious sites (cover shoulders and knees)
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Greet with a smile and a light bow; avoid touching heads
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Use ride-hailing apps like Grab or Gojek to avoid overcharging
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Desmond.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Don't leave valuables on the beach or in open bags
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Desmond.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Watch for monkeys near temples – keep sunglasses and phones secure
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Desmond.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Be cautious on wet terrain near waterfalls and cliffs
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Desmond.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Use Grab or Gojek apps for fair pricing. Taxis near tourist areas often overcharge or refuse to use meters
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Desmond.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Avoid unmarked taxis and drivers offering cheap day tours on the spot—always book through trusted providers or your hotel
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Desmond.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Only use authorized money changers with glass-walled counters and official signage
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Desmond.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Avoid roadside currency stalls offering high rates—they often shortchange or use sleight of hand
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Desmond.pdf

safety_tips.tip: At spots like Uluwatu, monkeys are notorious for grabbing sunglasses, phones, and hats. Avoid carrying visible food or loose accessories
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Desmond.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Credit cards are widely accepted in mid- to upscale venues, but watch for card skimming. Use cash or contactless payments when in doubt
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Desmond.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Use ride-hailing apps like Grab or Gojek to avoid overcharging
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali - All Inclusive Guide - Prashita.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Don't leave valuables on the beach or in open bags
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali - All Inclusive Guide - Prashita.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Watch for monkeys near temples – keep sunglasses and phones secure
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali - All Inclusive Guide - Prashita.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Be cautious on wet terrain near waterfalls and cliffs
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali - All Inclusive Guide - Prashita.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Use Grab or Gojek apps for fair pricing. Taxis near tourist areas often overcharge or refuse to use meters
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali - All Inclusive Guide - Prashita.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Avoid unmarked taxis and drivers offering cheap day tours on the spot—always book through trusted providers or your hotel
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali - All Inclusive Guide - Prashita.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Only use authorized money changers with glass-walled counters and official signage
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali - All Inclusive Guide - Prashita.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Avoid roadside currency stalls offering high rates—they often shortchange or use sleight of hand
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali - All Inclusive Guide - Prashita.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Buy SIMs inside the airport or from official telecom shops. Avoid kiosks with handwritten price lists or tourist SIMs with inflated prices
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali - All Inclusive Guide - Prashita.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Bargain politely in markets. If a price seems unreasonably high, walk away to find a better deal nearby
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali - All Inclusive Guide - Prashita.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Avoid paying before receiving the item or service (especially for clothing tailoring or craft pickups)
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali - All Inclusive Guide - Prashita.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Stick to spas recommended by hotels or trusted guides. Some budget massage parlors in high-tourist areas can be shady or upsell aggressively
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali - All Inclusive Guide - Prashita.pdf

safety_tips.tip: At spots like Uluwatu, monkeys are notorious for grabbing sunglasses, phones, and hats. Avoid carrying visible food or loose accessories
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali - All Inclusive Guide - Prashita.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Credit cards are widely accepted in mid- to upscale venues, but watch for card skimming. Use cash or contactless payments when in doubt
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali - All Inclusive Guide - Prashita.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Greet locals with a smile and a light bow (no handshake unless offered)
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali - All Inclusive Guide - Prashita.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Remove shoes when entering temples and private homes
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali - All Inclusive Guide - Prashita.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Dress modestly when visiting religious sites – cover shoulders and knees
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali - All Inclusive Guide - Prashita.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Use both hands when giving or receiving something (a sign of respect)
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali - All Inclusive Guide - Prashita.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Avoid touching anyone's head – it is considered sacred
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali - All Inclusive Guide - Prashita.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Don't point your feet at people or sacred objects
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali - All Inclusive Guide - Prashita.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Avoid public displays of affection, especially near temples
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali - All Inclusive Guide - Prashita.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Never climb on temple structures or altars for photos
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali - All Inclusive Guide - Prashita.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Keep valuables secure and out of sight in busy areas
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Pratheek_All Inclusive Guide_Bali.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Use a money belt or neck pouch for passports and extra cash
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Pratheek_All Inclusive Guide_Bali.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Carry copies of travel documents (digital and paper)
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Pratheek_All Inclusive Guide_Bali.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Avoid isolated alleys or beaches late at night
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Pratheek_All Inclusive Guide_Bali.pdf

safety_tips.tip: In mosques/churches: dress modestly and be respectful
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Pratheek_All Inclusive Guide_Bali.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Don't touch offerings (Canang Sari) on roads/temple steps
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Pratheek_All Inclusive Guide_Bali.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Don't raise your voice or show anger
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Pratheek_All Inclusive Guide_Bali.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Don't take selfies with your back towards temple idols
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Pratheek_All Inclusive Guide_Bali.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Don't enter sacred places without appropriate attire
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Pratheek_All Inclusive Guide_Bali.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Don't take coral, shells, or sand home — it's illegal
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Pratheek_All Inclusive Guide_Bali.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Don't litter; Bali is very strict about waste management
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Pratheek_All Inclusive Guide_Bali.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Beware of taxi scams with overcharging or broken meters
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Pratheek_All Inclusive Guide_Bali.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Be cautious of money exchange scams where they switch notes during counting
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Pratheek_All Inclusive Guide_Bali.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Watch for motorbike rental damage scams with unreasonable repair fees
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Pratheek_All Inclusive Guide_Bali.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Be aware of pickpocketing in crowded areas like Kuta, Seminyak nightlife, markets, and waterfalls
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Pratheek_All Inclusive Guide_Bali.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Use Grab or Gojek for transparent cab fares and avoid roadside price negotiations
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/All Inclusive Guide- Bali.docx

safety_tips.tip: Keep valuables secured — especially at beaches, waterfalls, and during boat transfer
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/All Inclusive Guide- Bali.docx

safety_tips.tip: At temples (especially Lempuyang), keep sunglasses, phones, and loose items secure — monkeys may grab visible objects
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/All Inclusive Guide- Bali.docx

safety_tips.tip: Wear slip-resistant footwear at wet terrain areas
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/All Inclusive Guide- Bali.docx

safety_tips.tip: Stay hydrated — Bali's humidity can cause dehydration quickly
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/All Inclusive Guide- Bali.docx

safety_tips.tip: Use ATMs located inside proper establishments (not standalone roadside machines)
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/All Inclusive Guide- Bali.docx

safety_tips.tip: Use ride-hailing apps like Grab or Gojek to avoid overcharging
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Priyankee and Varun.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Don't leave valuables on the beach or in open bags
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Priyankee and Varun.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Watch for monkeys near temples – keep sunglasses and phones secure
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Priyankee and Varun.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Be cautious on wet terrain near waterfalls and cliffs
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Priyankee and Varun.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Stay hydrated – Bali's climate can be warm and humid even in the evening
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Priyankee and Varun.pdf

safety_tips.tip: At spots like Uluwatu, monkeys are notorious for grabbing sunglasses, phones, and hats. Avoid carrying visible food or loose accessories
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Priyankee and Varun.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Beware of overcharging taxis—use Grab/Gojek where allowed
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Keep valuables close; use anti-theft bags in crowded areas
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Avoid isolated beaches after dark
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Always remove shoes when entering temples and homes
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Dress modestly in religious sites (cover shoulders and knees)
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide.pdf

safety_tips.tip: Greet with a smile and a light bow; avoid touching heads
safety_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Available providers include XL Axiata, Telkomsel, and Smartfren. Telkomsel is recommended as it has the best coverage and most advanced 4G/5G network, though it may be more expensive.
connectivity_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Travel Guide.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Best place to buy prepaid SIM cards is at Denpasar International Airport (DPS) on arrival. Look for tables after duty free or next to luggage belt 4 for Telkomsel cards, or in the main arrival hall after customs on the left side.
connectivity_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Travel Guide.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Recommended SIM Provider: Telkomsel (Best network and fastest speeds)
connectivity_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Where to Buy SIM: Denpasar Airport Arrival Hall – look for stalls near luggage belt 4 or after customs
connectivity_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Get your SIM on arrival for instant access to maps, ride-hailing apps, and translation tools
connectivity_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf

connectivity_tips.tip: Recommended Provider: Telkomsel for strong coverage and fast data speeds across Bali

connectivity_tips.tip: 15–20 GB plans available for ~IDR 100,000–150,000 (₹500–800)

Cab hailing apps available only in certain areas such as Nusa Dua, Kuta, Seminyak and Ubud
Book transport via hotel for greater flexibility
Ride-hailing app for fair pricing and reliable transport

transport_options.description: Most convenient and comfortable way to explore Bali, especially for full-day sightseeing, inter-city transfers, and early morning starts. Fixed pricing for the day, no navigation stress, air-conditioned comfort, door-to-door service
Perfect for short city rides, dining transfers, and casual outings. Best for Ubud café hopping and hotel to market transfers
Ride-sharing app for transparent cab fares
Ride-sharing app for transparent cab fares
Private transfers between locations and airport

health_tips.tip: Travel insurance recommended - TATA AIG is suggested. Carry doctor prescriptions/travel prescriptions for medicines except over the counter medications.
Recommended Vaccines: Hepatitis A, Typhoid, Tetanus
health_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf

health_tips.tip: Routine Immunizations: Ensure MMR, DPT, and Polio are up to date
health_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf

health_tips.tip: Medications to Carry: Paracetamol, Ibuprofen, Antihistamines, Anti-diarrheals, Motion sickness tablets, Mosquito repellents & creams
health_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf

health_tips.tip: Recommended travel insurance: TATA AIG
health_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Karthik.pdf

health_tips.tip: Recommended vaccines: Hepatitis A, Typhoid, Tetanus (ensure up to date), Routine immunizations: MMR, Polio, DPT
health_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Desmond.pdf

health_tips.tip: Carry medications: Paracetamol/Ibuprofen, Antihistamines, Anti-diarrheal tablets, Motion sickness pills, Mosquito repellent & bite cream
health_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Desmond.pdf

health_tips.tip: Recommended vaccines: Hepatitis A, Typhoid, Tetanus (ensure up to date), Routine immunizations: MMR, Polio, DPT
health_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali - All Inclusive Guide - Prashita.pdf

health_tips.tip: Carry medications: Paracetamol/Ibuprofen, Antihistamines, Anti-diarrheal tablets, Motion sickness pills, Mosquito repellent & bite cream
health_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali - All Inclusive Guide - Prashita.pdf

health_tips.tip: No vaccines are mandatory for entry, but routine vaccinations and a few travel-related vaccines are recommended
Tap water is safe to drink in most parts of Bali, but not recommended in many parts on some Bali islands
Be cautious with ice in drinks and unpeeled fruits from small vendors
Carry prescriptions in original packaging plus digital copy
Recommended vaccinations: Hepatitis A, Typhoid, Tetanus (ensure booster is up to date), Routine immunizations: MMR, Polio, DPT
Carry basic medications: Paracetamol / Ibuprofen, Antihistamines, Anti-diarrheal tablets, Motion sickness pills (for Nusa Penida boat transfer), Personal prescription medicines
Carry ORS / electrolyte sachets (helpful in humid weather)
Use mosquito repellent & bite cream
Bali is a safe destination health-wise, but basic precautions are advisable due to tropical weather, jungle terrain, and water-based activities
Recommended Vaccines: Hepatitis A, Typhoid, Tetanus (ensure up to date), Routine immunizations: MMR, Polio, DPT
health_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Priyankee and Varun.pdf

health_tips.tip: Carry medications: Paracetamol / Ibuprofen, Antihistamines (for allergies or insect bites), Anti-diarrheal tablets, Motion sickness pills, Mosquito repellent & bite cream
health_tips._source_file: Bali Indonesia/Bali Bliss All-Inclusive Guide - Priyankee and Varun.pdf

health_tips.tip: Recommended Vaccines: Hepatitis A, Typhoid, Tetanus
Routine Immunizations: Ensure MMR, DPT, and Polio are up to date
Carry Paracetamol, Ibuprofen, Antihistamines, Anti-diarrheals, Motion sickness tablets, Mosquito repellents & creams
